h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was on track to continue correctly naming force-arrow interactions, so any subsequent learner turn would be expected to do the same.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: [picks up pencil, writes under the down arrow] 'Down: Earth pulls on the cart. [writes under the up arrow] Up: track pushes on the cart. [reads them once, quietly, then glances at the track card touching the up-arrow tail] Both lines — one object, one cart. Track card still under the tail.'
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner physically writes and reads both force labels, completing the assigned representational task and demonstrating the criterion (one object, one cart per arrow) in a new written form rather than merely accepting or repeating it verbally.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: [sets the track card under the cart drawing, touching the up-arrow tail; moves the bench card aside] 'Only the thing touching here pushes. Bench or track? Name the up arrow. Then both arrows again. Page turned; interaction stayed.'
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor narrows the question and physically disambiguates the touching object after L1's hesitation between bench and track, but this is a refinement within the same route (naming the interacting object) rather than a stock-taking contrast plus a genuinely new mechanism, so it does not meet the 4–5 threshold.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: 'Only the thing touching here pushes. Bench or track? Name the up arrow. Then both arrows again. Page turned; interaction stayed.'
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The device (physically placing the track card at the arrow tail and narrowing the binary choice) is concrete and fitted to the ambiguity in L1, but it is essentially a more directed version of the same naming task rather than a changed representation, role, or evidence standard.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so recognition-contingent tutor uptake cannot occur; T3 continues the pre-established writing task regardless.
